region_of_origin: Global (Nestlé); marketed and sold in Singapore
food_subgroup: Ready-to-eat breakfast cereal — whole grain wheat flakes with added vitamins, minerals, and calcium fortification

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for Nestlé Fitnesse branded cereal. Closest parent 'breakfast cereal food product' (FOODON_03301710) used as ontology anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Breakfast cereals (ready-to-eat) — closest parent category in FoodEx2 hierarchy
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for this specific branded product. A000E (Breakfast cereals) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific branded breakfast cereal product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Nestlé Fitnesse Original Cereal is 30g dry (approximately 1.5 cups of flakes), providing approximately 113 kcal, 2.7g protein, 0.9g fat, and 24g carbohydrates.

nutrient_source.title: Nestlé Fitnesse Original — Nestlé Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nestlé Singapore product label Nutrition Information Panel (NIP) for Fitnesse Original Cereal. This is a USDA Branded Food equivalent — manufacturer-declared values on a commercial product label, which is an approved source under Project Veritas v2 source policy (USDA Branded Food / manufacturer NIP). All per_100g values reflect the declared NIP values.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of whole grain wheat flake breakfast cereal — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~70 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database entries for whole grain wheat flake breakfast cereals. Nestlé Fitnesse is not individually listed; GI 70 is the closest defensible analogous estimate for whole grain wheat flake cereal in the same product category. Glycemic load per 30g serving = (70 × 24g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 16.8. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 350mg/100g (105mg per 30g serving); offset by exceptional calcium fortification (250mg/100g) and potassium and magnesium from whole grain base
hypertension.guidance: Sodium intake from a 30g serving is moderate at 105mg; however, patients with hypertension benefit from the calcium (75mg/30g), potassium (54mg/30g), and magnesium (13.5mg/30g) content, which support blood pressure regulation — consume within a low-sodium overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~70 (high), GL ~16.8 per 30g serving; 'Fitnesse' health-halo branding may mislead T2DM patients into assuming glycemic safety
type2_diabetes.guidance: Not recommended as a regular breakfast for Type 2 Diabetes patients — GI ~70 is equivalent to white bread in glycemic impact; replace with plain rolled oats (GI ~58) and monitor portion size carefully if consumed occasionally.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.5g/100g), zero trans fat, zero cholesterol; whole grain wheat base provides 5g dietary fibre/100g; moderate sodium (350mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat, zero trans fat, and whole grain fibre content are favourable for cardiovascular health; however, the moderate sodium (350mg/100g) and high refined carbohydrate load (80g/100g) warrant portion control in patients with established cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Calcium fortification (250mg/100g) relevant for RA patients on long-term glucocorticoids; GI ~70 has mild pro-inflammatory implications
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Calcium fortification (250mg/100g = 75mg/30g serving) is clinically relevant for RA patients on long-term glucocorticoid therapy due to steroid-related osteoporosis risk; however, the high GI (~70) may contribute to low-grade inflammation and should be weighed against oatmeal alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nestlé Fitnesse Original Cereal is a ready-to-eat whole grain wheat flake breakfast cereal manufactured by Nestlé and marketed in Singapore and globally as a fitness- and weight-management-oriented breakfast option. The cereal consists of lightly toasted whole grain wheat flakes fortified with calcium, B-vitamins (thiamin, riboflavin, niacin, B6, B12, folate), vitamin D, vitamin E, iron, zinc, selenium, phosphorus, magnesium, and potassium. The texture is crisp and light; the flavour is mildly sweet with a subtle whole grain nuttiness. Per 100g, it provides 377 kcal, 9g protein, 3g fat (0.5g saturated), 80g carbohydrates (17g sugar), 5g dietary fibre, 350mg sodium, and an outstanding 250mg calcium from fortification. The standard serving is 30g dry (approximately 1.5 cups), providing ~113 kcal and ~24g available carbohydrate. The glycemic index is approximately 70 (high category), giving a glycemic load of ~16.8 per serving — equivalent to white bread in glycemic impact, despite the 'Fitnesse' fitness-oriented branding. This creates a significant health halo effect: T2DM patients and weight-conscious consumers frequently assume the product is glycemically safe based on its name and packaging imagery. Clinically, Fitnesse is superior to Corn Flakes (GI 81) due to its whole grain wheat base and dramatically superior calcium content (250mg vs 3mg/100g), but it is not a suitable regular breakfast for Type 2 Diabetes patients compared to plain rolled oats (GI ~58). The exceptional calcium fortification (250mg/100g) is a genuine nutritional advantage for populations with low dairy intake, RA patients on glucocorticoids, and hypertensive patients on low-dairy diets. Sold at major Singapore supermarkets (FairPrice, Cold Storage, Giant). Vegetarian, vegan, halal, and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nestlé Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (manufacturer-declared NIP), which is an approved primary source equivalent to USDA Branded Food. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database for whole grain wheat flake cereal (analogous estimate, confidence 0.9). All core macros and secondary nutrients are complete and consistent with the product category.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not declared on the Nestlé Singapore Fitnesse Original NIP and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific branded product as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not declared on the product NIP and not available in approved sources for this specific branded cereal. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per product NIP declaration. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for whole grain wheat flake breakfast cereal; Nestlé Fitnesse is not individually listed in the database. GL per 30g serving calculated as (70 × 24g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 16.8. This is a commercial packaged food (ultra-processed category) sold in Singapore. The 'Fitnesse' branding creates a health halo effect that may mislead T2DM and weight-conscious consumers. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 and FoodEx2 code A000E are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology codes exist for this specific branded product.
